state.interpretation: Selection is a scout queue only. Detailed remote canary eligibility is not field verification, publication approval, or a live-content claim.

selection_policy.goal: Smallest credible first canary scout packet: one neutral four-stop West Village architecture-and-public-space route plus one four-stop Greenwich Village reserve route.
selection_policy.admission: Detailed villages remote decision must be canary_eligible; low risk is required for this cohort; every item remains remote-only and scout-gated.
exclusions: Interior, purchase, operator-permission, or private-access dependencies
Any field-verified, published, or live claim
Sensitive memorials in the first recreational canary route; review separately under an explicit respectful editorial policy

walkability.basis: ordered straight-line distances derived only from source-bank coordinates; not a pedestrian route or access claim
ordered straight-line distances derived only from source-bank coordinates; not a pedestrian route or access claim

candidates.selection_rationale: Low-risk detailed remote decision and durable exterior architecture target support a compact neutral first-canary route; current viewpoint, access, obstruction, and safety conditions remain unverified.
candidates.observable_target_to_verify: Look up at the former New York Savings Bank and find the beehive emblem on its facade.

field_questions.access: Can the beehive and two-faced clock be seen from a legal public sidewalk position without stopping at the subway entrance?
field_questions.safety: Can completion use signalized crossings and avoid the curb, subway flow, and crowded corner pinch points?
field_questions.closure: Are scaffolding, trees, retail signs, construction, or temporary barriers obscuring the high facade detail?
field_questions.staleness: Are the carved beehive and projecting two-faced clock still present and uniquely observable from the intended viewpoint?

candidates.accessibility_evidence_note: Detailed remote review records step-free approach evidence, but launch accessibility remains unknown until a scout checks the actual completion viewpoint and route conditions.

pass_requirements: Target is currently present, uniquely resolvable, and observable exactly as scoped.
A legal public completion viewpoint exists without purchase, entry, touching, climbing, interference, or residential intrusion.
Scout records actual access, mobility, closure, obstruction, route, and safety observations with evidence references.
An authorized reviewer accepts the evidence before any field_verified transition or canary use.

replacement_pair.activation: explicit evidence review required; never automatic

disqualifiers: Target is missing, materially altered, covered, or not uniquely resolvable.
No legal public completion viewpoint exists without entry, purchase, touching, climbing, interference, or residential intrusion.
Current closure, construction, crowding, traffic, or barrier conditions make completion unsafe or impractical.
No workable completion experience exists for the observed mobility/access conditions.

candidates.selection_rationale: Low-risk detailed remote decision and durable triangular public-space geometry complete the compact northbound sequence; the clearest safe viewpoint, gates, and street crossings remain unverified.
candidates.observable_target_to_verify: Find the triangular park where Greenwich Avenue, Horatio Street, and Eighth Avenue meet.

field_questions.access: Can the triangular park geometry be recognized from a legal public park or sidewalk viewpoint during posted hours?
field_questions.safety: Can the stop be completed using marked crossings without standing in a roadway or traffic island?
field_questions.closure: Are gates, renovation fencing, foliage, or an event preventing the intended observation?
field_questions.staleness: Do Greenwich Avenue, Horatio Street, and Eighth Avenue still make the target uniquely resolvable on site?

disqualifiers: Target is missing, materially altered, covered, or not uniquely resolvable.

candidates.selection_rationale: Low-risk detailed remote decision and durable exterior park_garden target support a compact neutral first-canary route; current viewpoint, access, obstruction, and safety conditions remain unverified.
candidates.observable_target_to_verify: Find the quarter-acre park bordered by Eighth Avenue, Bank Street, Hudson Street, and West 12th Street.

field_questions.access: Can the quarter-acre footprint and four street edges be understood from a legal public sidewalk or step-free park entrance during posted hours?
field_questions.safety: Can completion avoid unsafe crossings, gate pinch points, and blocking park or sidewalk circulation?
field_questions.closure: Are locked gates, renovation, seasonal planting, an event, or temporary barriers preventing the intended observation?
field_questions.staleness: Do Eighth Avenue, Bank Street, Hudson Street, and West 12th Street still define the small park as scoped?

candidates.selection_rationale: Low-risk detailed remote decision and durable exterior architecture target support a compact neutral first-canary route; current viewpoint, access, obstruction, and safety conditions remain unverified.
candidates.observable_target_to_verify: Find the full-block artists’ complex named by combining West Street and Bethune Street.

field_questions.access: Can a public-facing Westbeth name marker and full-block footprint be observed from public perimeter sidewalks without entering residential space?
field_questions.safety: Can completion avoid photographing residents, lingering at entrances, or obstructing perimeter sidewalks and crossings?
field_questions.closure: Are facade work, scaffolding, temporary signs, or access restrictions obscuring the public-facing marker or block view?
field_questions.staleness: Is the Westbeth name visibly confirmable on site and is the West-plus-Bethune wordplay independently resolvable from public space?

candidates.selection_rationale: Low-risk detailed remote decision, permanent large exterior monument, and strong remote observability make this a robust reserve-route anchor; current barriers, crowding, and approach still need a scout.
candidates.observable_target_to_verify: At Fifth Avenue’s southern end, find the white marble triumphal arch built to honor George Washington.

field_questions.access: Is the arch fully observable from a legal public step-free park approach?
field_questions.safety: Can completion avoid climbing, crowd pinch points, and conflict with park circulation?
field_questions.closure: Is scaffolding, conservation work, an event, or a temporary barrier obscuring the arch?
field_questions.staleness: Does the white marble triumphal arch remain uniquely identifiable from the intended approach?

candidates.selection_rationale: Low-risk detailed remote decision, permanent central fountain structure, and proximity to other reserve stops support route resilience without depending on water operation; current access and obstruction require scouting.
candidates.observable_target_to_verify: At Washington Square Park’s center, find the circular Tisch Fountain; it need not be operating.

field_questions.access: Is the circular fountain structure observable from a legal public step-free park path?
field_questions.safety: Can completion avoid entering the basin, climbing, or obstructing the central gathering area?
field_questions.closure: Are maintenance, barriers, an event, snow, or crowding blocking the target?
field_questions.staleness: Is the circular Tisch Fountain uniquely identifiable even if it is not operating?

candidates.selection_rationale: Low-risk detailed remote decision, permanent exterior statue, and close proximity within the reserve loop provide target diversity; foliage, differentiation, and step-free sightline remain field questions.
candidates.observable_target_to_verify: Find the statue of Italian patriot Giuseppe Garibaldi in Washington Square Park.

field_questions.access: Is the Garibaldi statue visible from a legal public step-free park path during posted hours?
field_questions.safety: Can completion avoid climbing, touching, small-inscription reading, and interference with gatherings?
field_questions.closure: Are foliage, conservation barriers, an event, or snow materially obscuring the statue?
field_questions.staleness: Can a scout distinguish this statue from nearby monuments without relying on a small inscription?

candidates.selection_rationale: Low-risk detailed remote decision, durable fenced-court form, and proximity to Washington Square provide a reserve endpoint; active play, privacy, event, and perimeter conditions require scouting.
candidates.observable_target_to_verify: Find the compact fenced streetball court known as “The Cage”.

field_questions.access: Is the compact fenced court identifiable from a legal public step-free sidewalk viewpoint without entering the playing area?
field_questions.safety: Can completion avoid interrupting games, blocking gates, or photographing identifiable players?
field_questions.closure: Are resurfacing, an event, barriers, or dense activity preventing a non-interfering observation?
field_questions.staleness: Does the court remain plainly identifiable as the compact fenced West Fourth Street Courts target?
